"cvss_v4": {"score": 8.7, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:L/UI:N/VC:H/VI:H/VA:L/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-200", "name": "Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor"}, {"cwe_id": "CWE-266", "name": "Incorrect Privilege Assignment"}, {"cwe_id": "CWE-284", "name": "Improper Access Control"}], "description": "### Summary

OpenClaw hook ingress can start automated agent runs using a configured hook token. In affected releases, a hook-triggered run could select a bundled CLI backend that received owner-scoped MCP loopback authority instead of a scope appropriate for hook ingress.

This issue affects the boundary between hook-token automation and owner-only MCP tools. It does not affect deployments with hooks disabled.

### Affected configurations

This affects deployments where hooks are enabled, `/hooks/agent` is reachable with a valid hook token, and a bundled CLI backend can be selected for the hook-triggered run.

### Impact

A caller with the hook token could cause the spawned CLI runtime to see or call MCP tools that should have been owner-only. The practical impact depends on which MCP tools are available; the reported proof used persistent cron state as a representative owner-only action.

### Patched Versions

The first stable patched version is `2026.5.20`.

Fixed in the `2026.5.20` stable release.

### Mitigations

Upgrade to `openclaw@2026.5.20` or later. Keep hook tokens secret, restrict network access to hook endpoints, and disable hooks when they are not needed.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00281, "percentile": 0.19658}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-6fvr-66p3-3qj4", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-07-02T16:05:03Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-6fvr-66p3-3qj4", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-6fvr-66p3-3qj4"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-53814"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-06-11T21:16:23Z", "published_at": "2026-07-02T16:05:03Z", "references": ["https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/security/advisories/GHSA-6fvr-66p3-3qj4", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-53814", "https://www.vulncheck.com/advisories/openclaw-privilege-escalation-via-hook-triggered-cli-mcp-tool-authority", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-6fvr-66p3-3qj4"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/openclaw/openclaw/security-advisories/GHSA-6fvr-66p3-3qj4", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw", "summary": "OpenClaw: Hook-triggered CLI runs could receive owner MCP tool authority", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-07-02T16:05:04Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-6fvr-66p3-3qj4", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "2026.5.20", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "openclaw"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 2026.5.20"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: OpenClaw: Hook-triggered CLI runs could receive owner MCP tool authority
